---

title: "Carts Extraction"
author: "Fabricio Lennart"
date: "2026-04-03"
format: html
jupyter: python3
execute:
    echo: true
    warning: false
    message: false
---

## Overview

This notebook extracts cart data from the API to join with products:

* Extract carts from the public API
* Flatten the nested products array
* Load into a DataFrame
* Save to the Fabric Lakehouse

---

## Step 1: Extract Carts from API

```{python}
import requests as rq
import json

url = "https://dummyjson.com/carts"

def request_data(link: str) -> str:
    response = rq.get(link, timeout=10)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.text

data = request_data(url)

data_json = json.loads(data)
carts = data_json["carts"]
```

---

## Step 2: Flatten Cart Products

```{python}
cart_rows = []

for cart in carts:
    for product in cart["products"]:
        cart_rows.append({
            "cart_id": cart["id"],
            "user_id": cart["userId"],
            "product_id": product["id"],
            "product_title": product["title"],
            "quantity": product["quantity"],
            "line_total": product["total"]
        })

cart_rows[:3]
```

---

## Step 3: Load into DataFrame

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df_carts = pd.DataFrame(cart_rows)
df_carts.head()
```

---

## Step 4: Save to Lakehouse

```{python}
df_carts.to_csv('../data/carts.csv', index=False)
```

---

## Notes

* Each row represents one product inside a cart
* product_id is the key to JOIN with the products table
* user_id can be used later to join with a users table
